cases.question: Which companies in our database are independent — not owned by or part of any other company?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WHERE NOT EXISTS { (o)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-() }
RETURN o.name AS company
ORDER BY o.name

cases.question: How many news articles in our database are positive, neutral, or negative in tone? Give me a breakdown by sentiment category.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Article)
WITH
  WHEN a.sentiment > 0 THEN 'positive'
       a.sentiment < 0 THEN 'negative'
  ELSE 'neutral' END AS tone
RETURN tone, count(*) AS articleCount
ORDER BY articleCount DESC

cases.question: For the 5 most-covered companies in the news, what was the most positively-toned article written about each of them?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o:Organization)
WITH o, count(a) AS articleCount
ORDER BY articleCount DESC
LIMIT 5
CALL (o) {
  MATCH (best:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o)
  RETURN best
  ORDER BY best.sentiment DESC
  LIMIT 1
}
RETURN o.name AS company,
       articleCount,
       best.title AS topArticle,
       best.sentiment AS sentiment
ORDER BY articleCount DESC

cases.question: Which 5 companies have the most total connections in our database — counting ownership relationships, news mentions, and industry category links combined?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WITH o,
  COUNT { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization) } +
  COUNT { (:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(o) } AS ownershipLinks,
  COUNT { (:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o) }              AS mentionLinks,
  COUNT { (o)-[:IN_INDUSTRY]->(:IndustryCategory) } +
  COUNT { (o)-[:HAS_CATEGORY]->(:IndustryCategory) } AS industryLinks
WITH o,
  ownershipLinks + mentionLinks + industryLinks AS totalConnections,
  ownershipLinks,
  mentionLinks,
  industryLinks
ORDER BY totalConnections DESC
LIMIT 5
RETURN o.name        AS company,
       totalConnections,
       ownershipLinks,
       mentionLinks,
       industryLinks

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

-- embedding must be a 1536-element float array from your embedding model
-- (e.g. OpenAI text-embedding-3-small / text-embedding-ada-002)
-- No inline literal form is practical for a 1536-dim vector;
-- use the parametrized form and pass the vector at call time.
CYPHER 25
CALL db.index.vector.queryNodes('news', 5, $embedding) YIELD node, score
RETURN node.text AS segment, score

cases.question: For the 20 most-covered companies in our news database, show how many subsidiaries each owns and what the average tone of their press coverage is. Rank by most negative coverage first.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o:Organization)
WITH o, count(a) AS articleCount, avg(a.sentiment) AS avgSentiment
ORDER BY articleCount DESC
LIMIT 20
OPTIONAL MATCH (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization)
WITH o, articleCount, avgSentiment, count(sub) AS subsidiaryCount
ORDER BY avgSentiment ASC
RETURN o.name            AS company,
       articleCount,
       subsidiaryCount,
       round(avgSentiment, 3) AS avgSentiment

cases.question: Which company pairs appear in both positive and negative news stories? I'm looking for companies that generate mixed sentiment coverage together. Show the top 10 pairs.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o1:Organization),
      (a)-[:MENTIONS]->(o2:Organization)
WHERE o1.name < o2.name
WITH o1, o2,
     sum(CASE WHEN a.sentiment > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS positiveCount,
     sum(CASE WHEN a.sentiment < 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS negativeCount
WHERE positiveCount > 0 AND negativeCount > 0
RETURN o1.name AS company1, o2.name AS company2,
       positiveCount, negativeCount,
       positiveCount + negativeCount AS totalCoverage
ORDER BY totalCoverage DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

WHERE COUNT { (o)<-[:MENTIONS]-(:Article) } > COUNT { (o)<-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]-(:Organization) }

ORDER BY articleCount DESC

cases.question: Which pairs of companies in the same industry category are also covered together in the same news articles? Show the category, the company pair, and how often they appear together.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Organization)-[:IN_INDUSTRY]->(cat:IndustryCategory)<-[:IN_INDUSTRY]-(b:Organization)
WHERE elementId(a) < elementId(b)
MATCH (art:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(a), (art)-[:MENTIONS]->(b)
WITH cat.name AS category, a.name AS company1, b.name AS company2, count(art) AS coMentions
ORDER BY coMentions DESC
RETURN category, company1, company2, coMentions

cases.question: For companies that have at least one subsidiary, list each company's name alongside all the subsidiaries it directly owns and the industry categories it belongs to. Return up to 20 companies, ordered alphabetically.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (parent:Organization)
WHERE EXISTS { (parent)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization) }
RETURN parent.name AS company,
  COLLECT { MATCH (parent)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization) RETURN sub.name ORDER BY sub.name } AS subsidiaries,
  COLLECT { MATCH (parent)-[:IN_INDUSTRY]->(cat:IndustryCategory) RETURN cat.name ORDER BY cat.name } AS industries
ORDER BY parent.name
LIMIT 20

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: For 20 companies in our database, how many news articles mention each of them? Process the results in small batches.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WITH o LIMIT 20
CALL (o) IN TRANSACTIONS OF 5 ROWS {
  OPTIONAL MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o)
  RETURN o.name AS company, count(a) AS articleCount
}
RETURN company, articleCount
ORDER BY articleCount DESC

cases.question: By industry category, give me a comprehensive picture: how many companies are in each category, how many news articles mention them in total, what is the average sentiment of that coverage, and how many subsidiaries exist across the group? Show the top 10 categories by total article count.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (cat:IndustryCategory)<-[:IN_INDUSTRY|HAS_CATEGORY]-(o:Organization)
WITH DISTINCT cat, o
OPTIONAL MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o)
WITH cat, o, count(a) AS articleCount, avg(a.sentiment) AS orgSentiment
OPTIONAL MATCH (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(sub:Organization)
WITH cat, o, articleCount, orgSentiment, count(sub) AS subCount
RETURN cat.name AS category,
       count(o) AS companyCount,
       sum(articleCount) AS totalArticles,
       round(avg(orgSentiment), 3) AS avgSentiment,
       sum(subCount) AS totalSubsidiaries
ORDER BY totalArticles DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: For companies with at least 5 subsidiaries, show the most positively-covered news article about each (if any), and list the names of companies that sit two levels down in their ownership hierarchy. Return up to 10 companies.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (parent:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(child:Organization)
WITH parent, count(child) AS subCount
WHERE subCount >= 5
OPTIONAL MATCH (a:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(parent)
WITH parent, subCount, a
ORDER BY a.sentiment DESC
WITH parent, subCount, head(collect(a)) AS topArticle
OPTIONAL MATCH (parent)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(grandchild:Organization)
RETURN
  parent.name                       AS company,
  subCount                          AS subsidiaryCount,
  topArticle.title                  AS topArticleTitle,
  topArticle.sentiment              AS topArticleSentiment,
  collect(DISTINCT grandchild.name) AS grandchildren
ORDER BY subCount DESC
LIMIT 10

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Classify every company into one of four groups: those with both subsidiaries and press coverage, those with subsidiaries but no press coverage, those with press coverage but no subsidiaries, and those with neither. How many companies fall into each group?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (o:Organization)
WITH o,
  EXISTS { (o)-[:HAS_SUBSIDIARY]->(:Organization) } AS hasSubs,
  EXISTS { (:Article)-[:MENTIONS]->(o) }             AS hasPress
WITH CASE
       WHEN hasSubs AND hasPress          THEN 'Both subsidiaries and press coverage'
       WHEN hasSubs AND NOT hasPress      THEN 'Subsidiaries only (no press)'
       WHEN NOT hasSubs AND hasPress      THEN 'Press coverage only (no subsidiaries)'
       ELSE                                    'Neither'
     END AS group
RETURN group, count(*) AS companyCount
ORDER BY companyCount DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected
